feed.title: 4chan
feed.subtitle: Newest threads from selected worksafe 4chan boards (news, g, o, tv, v, mu, vip) via the read-only JSON API, plus the official 4chan blog. Board posts are user-generated and unmoderated; each title is prefixed with its board.

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: >LUCK stat in an rpg >Description: it does something, trust me bro ;) Name a sin…
entry.content: >LUCK stat in an rpg >Description: it does something, trust me bro ;) Name a single game with a satisfying LUCK stat.

entry.title: /int/ International: While I was walking the dog today I saw an african who looked like he'd just ste…
entry.content: While I was walking the dog today I saw an african who looked like he'd just stepped off the boat wearing his headcap, his flip-flops, his wifebeater and a crumbling backpack. He walked near me, spit on the ground and entered the local health center to enjoy his new free access to healthcare. Imagine being the doctor that has to treat him. I live in some random city in northern Spain. I frequently see africans walking around with backpacks when I go out now. I guess I'm gonna end up buying some
entry.dc:creator: /int/ International

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: Pretty much every artistic liberty Nolan took was kino. >Odysseus gets stuck on …
entry.content: Pretty much every artistic liberty Nolan took was kino. >Odysseus gets stuck on Calypso’s island because the lotus flowers suppressed his guilt. >Athena was a manifestation of his guilt over causing the downfall of Troy. >Sinon represents the innocent lives he had to sacrifice that he carried with him on his journey. >Helen is resentful about being used as propaganda to start this war. >Ex-Trojan War veterans like Odysseus were the Sea Peoples that caused the Bronze Age collapse. >The Odyssey is
entry.dc:creator: /tv/ TV & Film

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Let's face it: "AI" is going to be the most relevant technological topic in the …
entry.content: Let's face it: "AI" is going to be the most relevant technological topic in the coming years, but its fans make it impossible to have any intelligent discussions about it. Therefore any legitimate technology board will have to use "AI" to autoban "AI" fans.
entry.dc:creator: /g/ Technology

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: Is this the most profound utterance by an actor ever?
entry.content: Long from now, when you are an old man, dying in your bed, your kin will ask you, "Where were you when Pedro Pascal said he wants to be on the right side of history?"
entry.dc:creator: /tv/ TV & Film

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Goddamn it EU
entry.content: You had ONE job, you fucktards. https://hackaday.com/2026/07/17/eu- adds-exemptions-to-user-serviceable -batteries-rules/ >muh wet devices Look at the fucking Galaxy S5! >if its battery retains at least 83% of its original capacity after 500 charge cycles, battery replacement has to be only replaceable by professionals. Why such a random number? How much $$$$ did apple give you? Fuck you, you spineless bastards.
entry.dc:creator: /g/ Technology

entry.title: /int/ International: Do you have these in your country? It's a rug washing station. it exists in ever…
entry.content: Do you have these in your country? It's a rug washing station. it exists in every city here, several of them per city. It's owned and run by the city as a service. You can go a clean your rugs there for free. (but you need to bring your own soap) Do you have this kind of infrastructure in your country/city?
entry.dc:creator: /int/ International

entry.title: /g/ Technology: The rhetorical trick used by GrapheneOS is Reframing combined with a Red Herring…
entry.content: The rhetorical trick used by GrapheneOS is Reframing combined with a Red Herring (diversion). Instead of addressing the core issue (their compatibility failure with the Chase banking app) they shift the focus by aggressively redefining the critic's alternative. By weaponizing a False Dichotomy, they frame the choice solely between their OS and "dumbphones," which they paint as insecure and obsolete. They ignore the critic's actual advice (using web banking) to dodge their own product's flaws. Gr
entry.dc:creator: /g/ Technology

Every time there was a problem a fat woman talk about how hard it was on the News. Fat is not thicc or healthy. Fat is deadly, disgussting and repulsive. entry.title: /trash/ Off-Topic: /slop/ - AI Slop and Image Generation Thread #3017
entry.content: >Lovey-dovey Edition Fapping-focused AIslop thread for those who enjoy plapping to AI thread perfection. A thread for prompters, editors, requesters, gooners, AI pornmommies, PLAPpers, Stable Diffusion directors, NovelAI artists—anything and everything AIslop. No limits past site rules; use Catbox galleries and uploads if needed. Keep dumps to a minimum and don't be a derailing cunt, thanks. Previous Thread: https://desuarchive.org/trash/threa d/84133114/ >Scripts to check image metadata: rentry
entry.dc:creator: /trash/ Off-Topic

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Why space x will lose
entry.content: Yes space x is the most famous and competent space company in the world with space satellites and reusable rockets that cuts down the cost of going to space. That is why it will lose in less than a generation. All these bloated post colonial american companies are focused on QUALITY. Cost cutting is something Americans can never do. Quantity is an unfamiliar concept to americans. Quality is the only thing these exclusivity obsessed companies know how to do. That's why china will beat them if Spa
entry.dc:creator: /g/ Technology

entry.title: /s4s/ Sh*t 4chan Says: an apple a day probably wont keep the meaners away
entry.content: Nice apple
entry.dc:creator: /s4s/ Sh*t 4chan Says

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Why can't Europe, SK, Japan, Russia or India distill their own frontier models f…
entry.content: Why can't Europe, SK, Japan, Russia or India distill their own frontier models from American or Chinese A.I models? Why is the world forced between the two superpowers every single time? Why can't India or Europe step up and do what needs to be done? They clearly have the population, money, resources and talent, but for some reason hundred men teams in China can output SOTA models while entire continents struggle to. Are they just not trying?
entry.dc:creator: /g/ Technology

entry.title: /news/ News: US to Canada: Day of the Rake is Coming
entry.content: https://globalnews.ca/news/11968608 /wildfires-united-states-canada/ As wildfire smoke blankets parts of the country and spills into the United States, some U.S. lawmakers have sent a letter to the Canadian government saying that “sovereignty comes with responsibility.” Republican members of the U.S. House of Representatives Jack Bergman, John James, Lisa McClain and John Moolenaar penned a letter to Prime Minister Mark Carney, and reportedly sent it on Wednesday “demanding immediate action from
entry.dc:creator: /news/ News

entry.title: /b/ Random: OEDIPVS starring the Reiner family
entry.content: Now that the Odyssey dust has settled, we need to meme an Oedipus movie starring Nick Reiner into existence >Oedipus is a Greek tragedy about a man who, in his unsuccessful attempt to defy aprophecy, ends up fucking his mom and murdering his dad Imagine the kino if they brought Rob Reiner back as a CGI hologram, just so Nick can kill him again in glorious IMAX 3D Let’s make it happen /b/
entry.dc:creator: /b/ Random

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: >Cuz we ain't 'ad a proppa brawlin' chat in bleedin' ages! Me an' dat old git Au…
entry.content: >Cuz we ain't 'ad a proppa brawlin' chat in bleedin' ages! Me an' dat old git Aunt May don’t even know wot kinda Boy you are no more. You’z skivin' off yer proppa choppin' jobz! You got all dem weird, explosive humie gubbinz in yer cave! An' you'z startin' rumbles at da brain-nob-factory! >I didn’t start dat skrap, I toll' you already, ya git! >Well you zoggin' well finished it, didn't ya?! >Wot was I s'posed to do, leg it like a cowardly Grot?! >Nah, an Ork ain't s'posed to run away from a good

entry.title: /g/ Technology: are blu-rays the cheapest option for backups now? SSD = 200$ per TB HDD = 54$ pe…
entry.content: are blu-rays the cheapest option for backups now? SSD = 200$ per TB HDD = 54$ per TB Blu-ray = less than 27$ per TB also a SSD needs to be on once a year, HDD rewrite data at least every 5 years, meanwhile blu-ray should last up to 50 years without any baby sitting once the data is written
entry.dc:creator: /g/ Technology

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: >no challenge runs >no speedruns >no community events and contests (and gatherin…

entry.dc:creator: /v/ Video Games

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: Odyssey editing thread
entry.content: Ignore everything else about the movie, the raceswaps, the action, typical Nolan dialogue, can we just talk about the shitty editing? Not even 5 minutes in and I'm already getting frustrated. There's a cut after every single line of fucking dialogue, sometimes not even after a character is finished speaking. What the fuck is this?
entry.dc:creator: /tv/ TV & Film

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: league classic is saved

entry.dc:creator: /v/ Video Games

entry.title: /g/ Technology: What's the point of using AI "coding agents" to write the code for you
entry.content: Anybody can do that You may as well set it to code for you automatically and then do nothing for the rest of your life

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: >Christopher Nolan’s The Odyssey sells IMAX, not cinema. Billed as the biggest o…
entry.content: >Christopher Nolan’s The Odyssey sells IMAX, not cinema. Billed as the biggest of big movies (Titanic meets The Lord of the Rings with some Megalopolis thrown in), it continues the corruption of modern movie culture that Nolan started with The Dark Knight, which upped the scale of nihilistic violence to satisfy a hipster’s misconception of original filmmaking. Since then, he’s trained several generations to embrace mere action-spectacle as cinema, which, alone, can be stomach-turning. But it’s n
entry.dc:creator: /tv/ TV & Film

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: There's a scene where they sing along to some boomer rock song and I genuinely f…
entry.content: There's a scene where they sing along to some boomer rock song and I genuinely felt like a goy watching it and had to turn it off
entry.dc:creator: /tv/ TV & Film

entry.title: /g/ Technology: >After 2014: >Several flagship projects became less open or declined. >Venture c…
entry.content: >After 2014: >Several flagship projects became less open or declined. >Venture capital shifted attention away from community-driven hardware. >Open hardware continued to thrive in niches (scientific instruments, RISC-V, FPGA, embedded systems), but the broad cultural excitement of the early maker movement faded. https://wiki.opensourceecology.org/ wiki/History_of_Open_Source_Hardwar e Broothal.
entry.dc:creator: /g/ Technology

entry.title: /bant/ International/Random: Human life is a strange thing. People are not strong enough to live only for the…
entry.content: Human life is a strange thing. People are not strong enough to live only for themselves and die only for themselves. Human beings naturally seek something beyond themselves, an ideal, a purpose, or a noble cause to dedicate themselves to. If a person lives only for their own sake, they soon grow tired of it. And in the same way, when it comes to death, the idea of dying for something inevitably arises. In the past, that "something" was what we called a noble cause. To die for a noble cause was c
entry.dc:creator: /bant/ International/Random

entry.title: /bant/ International/Random: Going for groceries. What do you guys want?
entry.content: Going for groceries. What do you guys want?
entry.dc:creator: /bant/ International/Random

entry.title: /bant/ International/Random: What is this shit. What is this stupid bullshit. >Resetting back to safe mode in…
entry.content: What is this shit. What is this stupid bullshit. >Resetting back to safe mode in 12 minutes and 58 seconds. This shit comes up in my mind, every, single, day. Automatically. >Your brain needs to rest to finish building up new synapses Every single day this comes up, and every single day I skip sleep. You know why I skip sleeping? Because there's no way to rest without my brain resetting in the process. I would love to sleep, but there's no way to do it without getting these bullshit, meaningless
entry.dc:creator: /bant/ International/Random

entry.title: /bant/ International/Random: The internet has driven our species insane
entry.content: The last decade has convinced me we are simply not built for a world wide network of instant communication. The moment we developed tools our own evolution as a species slowed to a crawl with evolving technological advancements taking its place. We are still essentially an ape built for small, communal tribal environments of a handful of families working together to overcome challenges more simpler creatures can't find solutions to. That's our thing. Our niche. Our place in the natural order of
entry.dc:creator: /bant/ International/Random

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: God Of War’ To Recast Lead Kratos After Ryan Hurst Suffers Injury On Set Of Prime Video Series
entry.content: >This is a shocker: Prime Video‘s God Of War will have a new actor playing Kratos as the lead role in the high profile fantasy drama series is being recast, Deadline has learned. >The decision, made after a careful consideration by the studios on the show, Sony Pictures Television and Amazon MGM Studios, comes after Ryan Hurst, who had been originally cast as Kratos, was seriously injured on the set of the PlayStation video game adaptation in late June. https://deadline.com/2026/07/god-of -war-r
entry.dc:creator: /tv/ TV & Film

entry.content: >Cronus Frontier will ask a great deal of you in the days and months ahead. We're not soldiers or killers. We're scientists. Inventors. Explorers. And yet we will do whatever it takes to protect this colony and everything we've built here together. Not all of you are with us. Some of you have chosen to side with our enemies. Driven by fear. Greed. Ambition. I know who you are. And I know what you've done. Don't think you're getting out of this alive. What the fuck was Cantor's problem please tel

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: What was the first F2P MMO you remember playing, anons? For me it was probably G…
entry.content: What was the first F2P MMO you remember playing, anons? For me it was probably GunZ: The Duel or FlyFF. I never got into Maplestory growing up. If we're counting Runescape then it would definitely be Runescape for me. I remember playing that back in 2004. We had a massive MMORPG boom in 2007-2008, so I was playing so many during that year. Scions of Fate and 9Dragons for me. Mabinogi was another one I played on and off for like a year when it first launched.
entry.dc:creator: /v/ Video Games

entry.dc:creator: /bant/ International/Random

entry.title: /int/ International: Hazel eyes aren't just a simple blend of brown and green. They are a complex int…
entry.content: Hazel eyes aren't just a simple blend of brown and green. They are a complex interaction of melanin levels, collagen structures, and light scattering within the iris. Depending on lighting, hazel eyes may look more green, golden, or even greyish. This dynamic quality makes hazel eyes appear different every time you look at them - a living work of art. Only about 5% of the world's population has hazel eyes, making them rarer than blue eyes, but slightly more common than pure green or grey eyes. T
entry.dc:creator: /int/ International

entry.title: /int/ International: Did you know that Romanians use the words "cum" and "precum" in everyday speech?…
entry.content: Did you know that Romanians use the words "cum" and "precum" in everyday speech? "Cum" means "how" in Romanian, and "precum" means "such as."

entry.title: /g/ Technology: USB-C port not tight anymore?
entry.content: Here comes the savior. You take this little guy, slide him in between the middle plastic part holding the pins and the long edge of the port, then meticulously slide from one corner to another with dexterity to dislodge the lint or any other foreign matter. Blow and or pick out the debris and repeat until clean. Should be good as new.
entry.dc:creator: /g/ Technology

entry.content: Heaven gained an Angel edition >FTL News: --James Drake's camping trip, July 22nd --Bitchtank 2nd anniversary restream 7/19-8/1 --Ellie took her own life https://www.reddit.com/r/fishtankdo tlive/comments/1uybtw1/ellies_siste r_here/ >Links: https://www.fishtank.live/ https://x.com/fishtankislive https://www.instagram.com/fishtank. live/ https://fishtanklive.fandom.com/wik i/Fishtanklive_Wiki Previous: >>24675684

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Rust is the first AI-native language
entry.content: Why are the reddit luddites resisting this? I think it's long been obvious to everyone that any new project written in Rust is being developed entirely by AI. Rust is an AI-native language, and you should accept that fact. Rust projects don't have to be developed by humans. Even if the author doesn't know what their project does or why it was written, that doesn't make it bad.
entry.dc:creator: /g/ Technology

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: Why are there no good game journalists left?
entry.content: Why are there no good game journalists left?
entry.dc:creator: /v/ Video Games

entry.title: /b/ Random: i have schizoaffective disorder, it's hard to live with, i mean really fucking h…
entry.content: i have schizoaffective disorder, it's hard to live with, i mean really fucking hard. the mental part of it isn't that bad, but the emotional conflict is really awful, and has caused me a lot of trouble. i take lots of different drugs, to keep my mind off the way i feel. manic episodes are even worse than that feeling of crippling depression that comes around once in a while.
entry.dc:creator: /b/ Random
